fragility_points: A sharp crypto or macro risk-off move before Apr. 15 noon ET would hurt the Yes case.
Any confusion or operational issue around the exact Binance candle mapping could matter in a narrow contract.
key_assumptions: Current market pricing mainly reflects a volatility-adjusted spot anchor rather than a hidden bearish catalyst.
Traders are reading the ET-based Binance 1-minute close rule correctly.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market prior of 72.5% Yes.
Check Binance directly and confirm current spot is above 72k with recent 1-minute closes also above strike.
Check adjacent strike pricing and see a coherent implied distribution rather than an obvious stale misprice.
Keep probability close to market because narrow timing and BTC volatility preserve a meaningful No path.
main_thesis: The 72k Yes line at 72.5% looks broadly efficient because Binance spot is already above strike and the main remaining risk is short-horizon downside volatility into one exact noon ET minute.
quote_anchors: Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle final Close must be higher than 72,000.
Current price baseline: market-implied 72.5%, own estimate 74%.
source_quality_view: Source quality is medium-high overall: Binance is the governing primary source, Polymarket supplies the market prior, and contextual independence is moderate rather than high.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact 1-minute Binance close, so a temporary downside move can still flip the result.
Same-day contextual pricing near 71.2k earlier on Apr. 13 shows the required downside move is plausible.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT traded around 73.8k during the run, already above the 72k strike.
Adjacent Polymarket strikes were coherently spaced, suggesting the 72k line is not obviously stale.
timing_relevance: This is highly timing-sensitive because the market settles on the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at exactly 12:00 ET on 2026-04-15.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact pre-resolution volatility over the next ~42 hours remains unknown.
The ET-to-Binance candle mapping is clear enough to analyze but still worth explicit confirmation near settlement.
what_would_change_view: I would turn more cautious if Binance loses 72k and fails to recover, if downside volatility rises sharply, or if the exact resolution mechanics become less clean than they currently appear.
